economic_question: Does year-over-year growth in broad non-fund Regulation D capital-formation activity identify a risk-bearing state in which holding SPY improves the return/drawdown tradeoff versus a frozen monthly 50/50 SPY-cash benchmark?

signal.state_month: each calendar month m in quarter Q
signal.measured_population: initial non-fund Form D offerings; amendments and pooled investment funds excluded

portfolio.decision_at: 20:05 America/New_York on final accepted XNYS session before state month
portfolio.execution: first accepted XNYS open of state month
portfolio.holding: until next monthly execution open; unchanged state keeps existing exposure

measurement.decision_sizing_price: immediately preceding accepted XNYS raw close
measurement.interval_boundary: pretrade NAV marked at the immediately preceding accepted XNYS raw close
measurement.interval_return: successive pretrade boundary NAV ratio minus one
measurement.terminal_boundary: pretrade NAV at the session before the frozen terminal month execution
measurement.maximum_drawdown_path: initial capital followed by each accepted session opening NAV before the terminal boundary
measurement.cash_distributions: ex-date entitlement and pay-date settlement through the shared US Portfolio
measurement.spy_buy_and_hold: buy once at first validation or secondary execution and do not rebalance

splits.secondary_open_rule: only after all validation gates pass

method_limits: the current official workbook may revise historical statistics and no vintage archive is claimed
Form D is filer-reported and does not cover all exempt offerings
date-level aggregate statistics do not support a strict historical available_at claim
